registry_refs.notes: Two duplicate registry routes aggregate to this publication.

publication_scope.notes: The shared Harvard journals host contains many unrelated publications.

evidence.notes: Journal paths are allowed; Crawl-delay is 10 seconds.
One page lists Volume 1, Number 1 through Volume 20, Number 1; no pagination was present.
Gutenberg-era issue page. .wp-block-file elements link to full volume, front matter, and article PDFs; following paragraphs hold authors.
Legacy issue page: each entry is a paragraph containing a direct PDF link followed by author text.
Oldest listed issue, Winter 2007, uses the legacy paragraph layout.
1,124,720 bytes; begins %PDF-1.3. First-page text matches the linked article and names Ally Coll.

reconnaissance.notes: The site has no separate HTML article pages; issue pages link directly to article PDFs. This prevents the dossier from satisfying the schema's three-article-page ready gate even though one PDF smoke passed.

scope.notes: Issue pages mix article PDFs with front matter, mastheads, and whole-volume files; classify by link text before acquisition.

nodes.notes: Lists direct PDF links and issue-level metadata.

edges.notes: Route shapes vary, so follow observed links instead of synthesizing paths.
Filter link text to exclude Full Volume, Front Matter, and Masthead.

archive_patterns.notes: Some issues use /print-archive/, some /hlprvolumes/, and some the publication root.
Upload year is not reliable for migrated legacy issues.

pagination.termination: The single archive page reaches Volume 1, Number 1 and exposes no next link.
pagination.notes: Navigation-menu duplicates should be deduplicated by normalized URL.

layout_eras.label: Legacy paragraph issue layout
layout_eras.notes: PDF link and author text share one paragraph.

layout_eras.label: Gutenberg file-block issue layout
layout_eras.notes: Each PDF uses .wp-block-file and its author is in the following paragraph. Intermediate unsampled years may contain either layout.

sources.example: Redefining Efficiency in the DOGE Era: The Value of Equitable Evidence-based Policymaking in Federal Agencies

title.missingness: Present for all sampled article links.

sources.notes: Legacy layout requires separating title-link text from trailing author text.

authors.missingness: Some legacy entries, including one sampled issue, place an author in the next paragraph.
authors.notes: Use PDF first-page text as a verification fallback.

sources.locator: first pages following the article title and author
sources.example: In one of the first actions of his second term...
sources.notes: No abstract was present in issue HTML.

abstract.missingness: Requires PDF text and may be absent by document type.

sources.notes: For current pages, corroborate with the file URL or PDF.

year.missingness: Observed on all sampled issues after issue-level parsing.
year.notes: Do not use upload year alone for migrated legacy files.

date.missingness: The newest sampled page did not show a full publication date.

volume.missingness: Present in all sampled issue URLs.

issue.missingness: Present in all sampled issue URLs.

pages.missingness: No page range was exposed in sampled issue HTML.

doi.missingness: No DOI was observed in sampled HTML or the smoke PDF.
doi.notes: Record null unless later evidence supplies one.

citation.missingness: No recommended citation block was present in sampled issue HTML.
citation.notes: Construct only downstream from verified metadata.

patterns.notes: One normal GET returned an article PDF directly.

patterns.locator: a[href$='.pdf'] with link text Full Volume

validation.notes: The smoke file began %PDF-1.3, returned application/pdf, and first-page text matched the article rather than a whole issue.

robots.notes: Respect Crawl-delay: 10 and recheck before acquisition.

access.politeness_notes: Use the /lpr/ publication prefix, deduplicate URLs, and wait at least 10 seconds between requests to the shared Harvard journals host.

verdict.summary: All listed HLPR issues are enumerable and direct article PDFs work across two observed layouts, but the site has no HTML article pages and some metadata requires issue-text or PDF parsing.
blockers: No HTML article-detail pages for the three-page dossier ready gate
Pages and citations are not exposed in issue HTML
verdict.next_action: Implement the two issue-layout extractors, classify non-article PDFs by link text, and run a one-target adapter smoke.
verdict.implementation_notes: The host is already mapped to WordPressAcademicBaseAdapter. Preserve the archive's observed URLs rather than synthesizing one route shape.
